acceptance_criteria: Storage abstraction round-trips (read/write/list) against a sibling github repo using platform-router credentials.
Schema exposes an optional explicit auth override but defaults to platform-router reuse — no new secret plumbing in the common path.
description: Introduce the `research:` block in `.polyphony-config/profile.yaml` (schema + validation) and a storage abstraction for the sibling research repo. Auth defaults to reusing the platform-router's existing credential resolution (gh/az); the schema accepts an optional explicit auth reference for cross-platform cases without breaking the default path. No new secret plumbing in the common case.
title: `research:` profile config + storage abstraction (cross-platform remote, auth via platform-router reuse)

acceptance_criteria: Sub-workflow accepts a `research_needs` payload and returns a synthesized findings document, end-to-end, callable from a test harness.
`research-assistant` sources strictly from the archive on this first pass (no live tools).
`research_needs` payload schema is defined in this issue and consumed unchanged by Issue 5.
description: Implement the research sub-workflow skeleton and the `research-assistant` agent (archive-only, cheap tier). Define the `research_needs` payload schema as part of this issue so downstream consumers (Issue 5) bind to a stable contract.
title: Research sub-workflow + `research-assistant` (archive-only first pass)

acceptance_criteria: Archive-insufficient cases escalate to the `deep-researcher` tier with extended tools (web/GitHub/MCP).
Escalation cap defaults to 1 per `research_needs` topic and is overridable via the `research:` profile.
Findings cite both archive and live sources when the deep tier is invoked.
description: Add the `researcher` sufficiency-judge step and the `deep-researcher` escalation tier (web/GitHub/MCP tools) inside the sub-workflow loop. Escalation cap defaults to 1 per `research_needs` topic and is overridable via the `research:` profile.
title: `researcher` sufficiency check + `deep-researcher` escalation (extended tools, default cap = 1)

acceptance_criteria: Every research call ends with an archivist keep/discard/expand decision.
Kept articles land in the sibling research repo with Johnny-Decimal layout and frontmatter.
`INDEX.md` is updated as part of the curation write.
description: Append an `archivist` curation step at the end of every research call (keep / discard / expand) and wire the sibling-repo writer using the storage abstraction from Issue 1. Kept articles land with Johnny-Decimal layout and frontmatter; `INDEX.md` is updated.
title: `archivist` curation pass + sibling-repo write

acceptance_criteria: An architect can emit `research_needs` and the workflow runs the research sub-workflow.
The next architect iteration receives the findings without traversing the plan-review gate.
A harness / dogfood scenario demonstrates the full architect → research → architect loop on a non-trivial prompt without human intervention.
description: Wire the architect agent to emit `research_needs`, run the research sub-workflow, and feed findings back into the next architect iteration without traversing the plan-review gate. Includes a harness / dogfood scenario that proves the full loop end-to-end on a non-trivial prompt.
title: Architect integration: `research_needs` emission + loop-back routing bypassing review

acceptance_criteria: Kept articles validate against the declared frontmatter schema.
Every wiki-link target resolves to an existing article in the sibling repo.
Citations include URL and capture date.
`INDEX.md` regenerates deterministically (byte-identical for the same input set).
description: Tighten the kept-article surface so the accumulated knowledge is mechanically discoverable for the next run: Johnny-Decimal numbering, Obsidian-compatible frontmatter and wiki-links, citation rigor (URL + capture date minimum), and deterministic `INDEX.md` regeneration. Validation is structural (CI-checkable), not GUI-dependent.
title: Wiki polish: Johnny-Decimal numbering, Obsidian frontmatter / wiki-links, citation rigor, `INDEX.md` maintenance
